## Summary

> Provide a short summary of the proposed standard or process change.

## Problem Statement

> What concrete ecosystem problem exists today?

## Why this needs an ARC

> Explain why this should be standardized instead of being handled as:
> - an application-specific feature,
> - an SDK or library change,
> - an implementation detail,
> - an amendment to an existing ARC.

## Existing ARCs and Related Work

> List the ARCs and external work reviewed before proposing this one.

## Affected Stakeholders

> Who would need to implement, support, or understand this proposal?
> Examples:
> - wallets
> - explorers
> - SDKs
> - indexers
> - dApps
> - tooling vendors

## Sponsor Classification

> Choose one:
> - `Foundation`
> - `Ecosystem`

## Reference Implementation Requirement

> Does this proposal require a canonical reference implementation?
> - `true`
> - `false`

> If `true`, describe the expected repository owner, maintainer, and likely implementation
> location.

## Adoption Hypothesis

> Who is likely to adopt this proposal? Name likely implementer classes or projects
> where possible.

## Alternatives Considered

> What alternatives were considered and why were they rejected?

## Outcome

> This will be provided by the ARC editor after discussion:
> 1. Status:
>    - `accepted-for-draft`,
>    - `rejected-no-arc-needed`,
>    - `redirected-to-existing-arc`,
>    - `recast-as-meta`.
> 1. ARC number.
